Scenario: Verify list of available restore points is displayed
Steps:
1. Navigate to Restore
2. Select a backup plan
ExpectedResult: All available restore points listed with date and status

Scenario: Verify restore points are sorted by date descending
Steps: 1. Open restore point list
ExpectedResult: Most recent restore point shown first

Scenario: Verify specific restore point can be selected
Steps:
1. Open restore point list
2. Click on a restore point to select it
ExpectedResult: Restore point selected — restore button becomes active

Scenario: Verify restore point details show file count and size
Steps:
1. Select a restore point
2. Check detail panel
ExpectedResult: File count and backup size shown correctly

Scenario: Verify filtering restore points by date range works
Steps:
1. Apply date filter to restore point list
2. Check results
ExpectedResult: Only restore points within selected range shown

Scenario: Verify restore from BIM 360 backup point can be initiated
Steps:
1. Select a BIM 360 restore point
2. Choose restore target
3. Click Start Restore
ExpectedResult: Restore starts — status shows Running

Scenario: Verify BIM 360 restore completes successfully
Steps:
1. Run full restore from BIM 360 backup
2. Wait for completion
ExpectedResult: Status = Completed — all files restored

Scenario: Verify BIM 360 restore can target original location
Steps:
1. Select restore point
2. Choose Restore to Original Location
ExpectedResult: Files restored to their original BIM 360 path

Scenario: Verify restore from Egnyte backup point can be initiated
Steps:
1. Select an Egnyte restore point
2. Choose restore target
3. Click Start Restore
ExpectedResult: Restore starts — status shows Running

Scenario: Verify Egnyte restore completes successfully
Steps:
1. Run full restore from Egnyte backup
2. Wait for completion
ExpectedResult: Status = Completed — all files restored to target

Scenario: Verify restore from Amazon S3 backup executes correctly
Steps:
1. Select restore point backed up to Amazon S3
2. Start restore
ExpectedResult: Restore completes — files retrieved from S3

Scenario: Verify restore from File System backup executes correctly
Steps:
1. Select restore point backed up to File System
2. Start restore
ExpectedResult: Restore completes — files retrieved from file system path

Scenario: Verify restore from S3 Compatible backup executes correctly
Steps:
1. Select restore point backed up to S3 Compatible
2. Start restore
ExpectedResult: Restore completes — files retrieved correctly

Scenario: Verify restore from Azure Blob Storage backup executes correctly
Steps:
1. Select restore point backed up to Azure Blob
2. Start restore
ExpectedResult: Restore completes — files retrieved from blob container

Scenario: Verify partial restore (specific folders only) works
Steps:
1. Select restore point
2. Choose specific folders to restore
3. Start restore
ExpectedResult: Only selected folders restored — others untouched

Scenario: Verify restore can be cancelled while running
Steps:
1. Start restore
2. Click Cancel
ExpectedResult: Restore stops — partial results noted

Scenario: Verify restored files match original files
Steps:
1. Complete a restore
2. Compare restored files to originals
ExpectedResult: File names sizes and structure match original backup

Scenario: Verify file metadata preserved after restore
Steps:
1. Complete restore
2. Check file metadata at destination
ExpectedResult: Created/modified dates and metadata match original

Scenario: Verify restore report shows correct success/fail counts
Steps:
1. Complete a restore
2. Open restore report
ExpectedResult: Success and failed file counts match actual results

Scenario: Verify failed restore files listed with error reason
Steps:
1. Run restore with some failures
2. Check report
ExpectedResult: Each failed file shows reason for failure
